topic: Khao Manee: Thailand's diamond-eye cat once owned by kings only
key_points: Khao Manee originated in Thailand and appears in the 14th-century Tamra Maew (Cat-Book Poems)
Pure white coat with one blue and one gold eye is the most prized variant called odd-eyed
Was reserved for Thai royalty and considered bad luck if owned by commoners
TICA accepted Khao Manee for championship status in 2015

topic: Singapura: smallest cat breed weighs less than 4 kg adult
key_points: Singapura adults weigh 1.8 to 3.6 kg according to TICA breed standard
Coat is sepia-toned ticked tabby, a single accepted color called sepia agouti
Originated from Singapore street cats imported to USA in 1975
Lifespan averages 11 to 15 years per CFA breed data

topic: LaPerm: curly-coated breed born from a single Oregon barn cat 1982
key_points: LaPerm originated in 1982 in The Dalles, Oregon from a hairless kitten named Curly
Curls are caused by a dominant gene affecting the hair shaft
Coat ranges from soft waves to corkscrew ringlets and can shed during kittenhood
Recognized by TICA as championship breed in 2002

topic: Donskoy: Russian hairless cat with retractable claws like kangaroo
key_points: Donskoy originated in Rostov-on-Don, Russia in 1987 from a rescued kitten
Hairlessness is caused by a dominant gene unlike Sphynx recessive gene
Skin is wrinkled and warm to touch, requires weekly bathing per breed clubs
TICA accepted Donskoy for championship in 2005

topic: Selkirk Rex: only cat breed with three coat types in same litter
key_points: Selkirk Rex originated in Montana in 1987 from a curly-coated rescue kitten
Single litter can produce straight, wavy, and curly coats due to incomplete dominance
Both shorthair and longhair varieties accepted by CFA since 2000
Whiskers also curl, a unique breed trait

topic: Korat: silver-blue Thai cat that legend says brings good fortune
key_points: Korat originated in Phimai province, Thailand and dates to 14th century
Coat is silver-tipped blue with no other color accepted in show standard
Eyes are luminous green, fully developed only at age 2 to 4 years
CFA accepted Korat for championship in 1966

topic: Burmilla: accidental UK breed from a Burmese-Chinchilla mix in 1981
key_points: Burmilla originated in 1981 in the UK from an accidental mating between Burmese and Chinchilla Persian
Coat is silver-shaded or silver-tipped with dark eye liner appearance
Eyes are green, large and distinctive
GCCF granted championship status in 1997

topic: Why all orange cats are usually male: X chromosome genetics explained
key_points: Orange coat color is controlled by the O gene located on the X chromosome
Males have only one X so a single O gene produces fully orange coat
Females need two copies of O to be fully orange, making them only 20 percent of orange cats
Confirmed by UC Davis Veterinary Genetics Laboratory research

topic: Calico cats are 99.9% female: rare male calicos have Klinefelter XXY
key_points: Calico pattern requires both orange and black genes on X chromosomes
Males with calico coloring have XXY chromosomes called Klinefelter syndrome
Klinefelter male calicos are typically sterile per UC Davis genetics data
Estimated occurrence is 1 in 3000 calico cats per Cornell Vet College

topic: Slow blink cat language: Sussex 2020 study proves it works
key_points: University of Sussex 2020 study published in Scientific Reports confirmed slow blink communication
Cats are more likely to approach humans who slow-blink at them first
Slow blink involves narrowing eyes followed by partial or full eye closure
Acts as cat positive emotional signal similar to a smile

topic: Cat ear positions decoded: 180 degree rotation and 32 muscles
key_points: Each cat ear has 32 muscles compared to 6 muscles in human ear
Cats can rotate ears independently up to 180 degrees
Ears flat against head signals fear or aggression per Cornell Vet behavior research
Ears forward and erect indicates alert and curious state

topic: Cat tail vocabulary: 9 specific positions and meanings explained
key_points: Tail held straight up signals confidence and friendly greeting per ASPCA behaviorist guides
Puffed tail indicates fear or threat response with piloerection
Slow side-to-side swish often precedes hunting or pouncing
Tail tucked under body signals submission or stress

topic: Cats see UV light invisible to humans: 2014 City University study
key_points: City University London 2014 study found cat lens transmits UV light
Humans block UV light with the lens, cats let it pass to retina
UV vision may help cats see urine trails left by prey on grass
Published in Proceedings of the Royal Society B

topic: Cats hear up to 64 kHz: highest range of any domestic mammal
key_points: Cat hearing ranges from 48 Hz up to 85 kHz per Heffner research at University of Toledo
Dogs hear up to 45 kHz, humans only up to 20 kHz
High-frequency hearing helps detect rodent ultrasonic vocalizations around 50 kHz
Cat ear can localize sound source within 3 inches at 1 yard distance

topic: Cats have 200 million scent receptors: 14 times more than humans
key_points: Cats have approximately 200 million olfactory receptors per Cornell Feline Health Center
Humans have about 5 million for comparison
Cats also have a vomeronasal organ called Jacobson's organ on roof of mouth
Flehmen response with open mouth helps process pheromone signals

topic: FeLV vs FIV: two cat retroviruses every owner should know about
key_points: Feline leukemia virus (FeLV) spreads via saliva sharing and grooming per AAFP guidelines
Feline immunodeficiency virus (FIV) spreads mainly through deep bite wounds
FeLV vaccine is core for outdoor cats per AAFP 2020 vaccination guidelines
FIV-positive cats can live full lives indoors with proper care per Cornell Vet

topic: Chronic kidney disease affects 30% of cats over 15: early signs
key_points: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) affects 30 percent of cats over 15 years per Cornell Feline Health Center
Early signs include increased thirst, increased urination, and weight loss
Diagnosis uses SDMA blood test which detects kidney loss earlier than creatinine
Phosphorus-restricted prescription diets slow disease progression per AAFP guidelines

topic: Senior cat behavior changes: when to worry vs normal aging
key_points: Cognitive dysfunction syndrome (CDS) affects over 50 percent of cats over 15 per Cornell research
Signs include nighttime vocalization, disorientation, altered sleep cycles
Sudden changes warrant vet exam to rule out hyperthyroidism or pain
Environmental enrichment slows cognitive decline per AAFP senior care guidelines

topic: Domestic cats descended from one African wildcat 9000 BCE Cyprus
key_points: All domestic cats descend from the African wildcat (Felis lybica) per 2017 Nature Ecology study
Earliest evidence of cat-human burial dates to 7500 BCE in Cyprus
Domestication occurred independently in Egypt and Near East per genetic data
Egyptian domestic line spread across Mediterranean by Roman era

topic: Larry the cat: 10 Downing Street's official mouser since 2011
key_points: Larry has held title of Chief Mouser to the Cabinet Office since 11 February 2011
Adopted from Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London
Has served under Cameron, May, Johnson, Truss, Sunak and Starmer governments
Official UK government salary is paid for from official donations not public funds

topic: Tama the stationmaster: Japanese cat that saved a railway line
key_points: Tama was appointed honorary stationmaster of Kishi Station, Wakayama Japan in 2007
Visitor numbers rose 17 percent and brought 1.1 billion yen to local economy per Wakayama Electric Railway
Wore official stationmaster cap during her duties
Funeral in 2015 was attended by 3000 people

topic: Cats dream like humans: REM sleep proven by MIT 1959 research
key_points: MIT researcher Michel Jouvet documented REM sleep in cats in 1959
Cats spend about 25 percent of sleep in REM phase per Tufts veterinary research
Twitching whiskers and paws during sleep indicate REM dreaming
Adult cats sleep 12 to 16 hours daily per Cornell Vet College

topic: Critical socialization window: 2-7 weeks shapes cat personality forever
key_points: Sensitive socialization period is 2 to 7 weeks per Karsh and Turner 1988 research
Kittens handled by humans during this window become friendlier adults
Lack of human contact before 7 weeks raises feral risk per ASPCA guidelines
Just 15 minutes of daily handling boosts sociability long-term

topic: Black cats more friendly? Coat color personality myth busted
key_points: UC Davis 2015 study found no significant link between coat color and personality
Owner perception bias drives myth, not actual behavior data
Black cats wait longest in shelters per ASPCA adoption statistics
Earlier 2012 UC Davis study showed slight aggression in tortoiseshells but limited sample

topic: Kitten eyes open at 7-10 days: full developmental timeline week by week
key_points: Kittens are born with eyes closed and ear canals sealed per ASPCA development chart
Eyes open between 7 and 10 days, ear canals open by 14 days
Weaning starts at 4 weeks with kitten food per Cornell Feline Health Center
Full weaning completed by 8 to 10 weeks of age

topic: Kitten vaccine schedule: when to start FVRCP and rabies shots
key_points: FVRCP core vaccine starts at 6 to 8 weeks per AAFP 2020 vaccination guidelines
Boosters given every 3 to 4 weeks until 16 to 20 weeks of age
Rabies vaccine given once at 12 to 16 weeks per AVMA recommendations
FeLV vaccine recommended for outdoor or multi-cat homes

topic: Cat purr frequency 25-150 Hz heals bones: NASA studied frequencies
key_points: Cat purrs range 25 to 150 Hz per UC Davis bioacoustics research
Frequencies 25 to 50 Hz promote bone density per orthopedic vibration studies
Cats purr while injured, suggesting self-healing function
NASA studied similar low-frequency vibrations for astronaut bone loss prevention

topic: Sophia University Tokyo 2019: cats know their own names
key_points: Sophia University Tokyo 2019 study confirmed cats distinguish their names from other words
Published in Scientific Reports by Atsuko Saito et al
Cats responded with ear or head movement to own name even from strangers
Effect held even when names had similar phonetic structure to control words

topic: Cat whiskers measure body width: how a cat fits through any gap
key_points: Whiskers (vibrissae) typically extend the width of the cat's body per Cornell Vet
Each whisker is rooted in a follicle rich with nerves and blood vessels
Cats use whiskers to assess if they can fit through openings
Whisker fatigue from food bowl edges may cause eating reluctance per Royal Canin research

topic: Cat dental disease affects 70%: how to spot it before tooth loss
key_points: Up to 70 percent of cats over 3 years have dental disease per AVDC American Veterinary Dental College
Tooth resorption is the most common dental issue in cats
Signs include drooling, pawing at mouth, eating on one side
Annual dental cleaning under anesthesia recommended by AVMA

topic: Cats taste no sweet: missing TAS1R2 gene explains zero candy interest
key_points: Cats lack functional TAS1R2 gene needed to detect sweet taste per Monell Chemical Senses Center 2005
All Felidae species share this genetic mutation, including lions and tigers
Cats have only about 470 taste buds vs 9000 in humans
This explains obligate carnivore preference per AAFCO nutrient profiles

topic: Taurine deficiency kills cats: AAFCO requires 0.1% in dry food
key_points: Cats cannot synthesize taurine and need it from diet per AAFCO nutrient profiles
Taurine deficiency causes dilated cardiomyopathy and retinal degeneration
AAFCO requires minimum 0.1 percent taurine in dry food, 0.2 percent in wet
1987 UC Davis research by Pion linked taurine to feline heart disease

topic: Hyperthyroidism strikes 10% of senior cats: weight loss despite hunger
key_points: Hyperthyroidism affects about 10 percent of cats over 10 years per Cornell Feline Health Center
Caused by benign thyroid tumors producing excess thyroid hormone
Classic signs are weight loss with increased appetite, vomiting, hyperactivity
Treatment options include radioactive iodine, daily methimazole, or thyroidectomy per AAFP

topic: Munchkin cat short legs: dwarfism gene controversy in cat shows
key_points: Munchkin breed has autosomal dominant gene causing achondroplasia-like dwarfism
TICA accepted Munchkin in 1995, but GCCF and FIFe refuse recognition over welfare concerns
Homozygous Munchkin embryos do not survive per Solveig Pflueger genetic studies
Spinal and joint issues remain debated in veterinary literature

topic: Norwegian Forest Cat: Viking ship cat breed nearly extinct in 1930s
key_points: Norwegian Forest Cat (Skogkatt) appears in Norse mythology pulling Freya's chariot
Nearly went extinct during World War II due to interbreeding with shorthairs
Breed club Norsk Skogkattring formed in 1975 to preserve pure lines
Double waterproof coat adapted to Scandinavian climate per FIFe standard

topic: Maine Coon largest domestic breed: average male 8.2 kg per CFA
key_points: Maine Coon males weigh 6 to 11 kg per CFA breed standard
Stewie holds Guinness record at 123 cm long, recorded 2010
Breed origins traced to ship cats in colonial Maine 1800s
Lifespan averages 12 to 15 years per Royal Canin breed data

topic: Polydactyl cats: extra toes from Hemingway colony in Key West
key_points: Polydactyly is autosomal dominant trait, normal cats have 18 toes total
Hemingway House in Key West maintains 60 polydactyl descendants of Snow White
Extra toes can reach up to 28 per cat per Guinness records
Caused by Hw mutation on chromosome A1 per UC Davis genetics

topic: Cats are obligate carnivores: why vegan cat food is dangerous
key_points: Cats require preformed vitamin A from animal sources per AAFCO nutrient profiles
Cannot convert beta-carotene to vitamin A like humans can
Need arachidonic acid only found in animal fat, not plants
AVMA position statement opposes plant-only cat diets due to nutritional risk

topic: Cat allergies in humans: protein Fel d 1 from saliva not fur
key_points: Fel d 1 protein produced in cat saliva and skin causes most allergies per Indoor Biotechnologies
Spread to fur during grooming, then airborne when fur sheds
Affects 10 to 20 percent of adults globally per AAAAI estimates
Purina LiveClear diet binds Fel d 1 in saliva, reducing allergen by 47 percent in 2020 study

topic: Why cats knead with paws: kitten nursing reflex into adulthood
key_points: Kneading behavior originates in nursing kittens stimulating milk flow
Adult cats knead when content per ASPCA behavior guides
Releases pheromones from paw scent glands marking territory
May knead before sleep as nesting comfort behavior

topic: Cats bring dead prey home: not gifts but teaching behavior
key_points: Female cats bring prey to teach kittens to hunt per ASPCA behavior research
Behavior persists in spayed females with no kittens, suggesting innate maternal trigger
Indoor cats may use toys as substitute prey delivery
Not motivated by giving gift to humans per Cornell behavior literature

topic: Cat zoomies after litter box: sciatic nerve and vagal stimulation theory
key_points: Post-defecation frenetic random activity periods (FRAPs) common in cats and dogs
Vagal nerve stimulation during defecation may cause euphoria per Cornell Vet research
Sudden zoomies could also signal medical issues like UTI or pain
Persistent post-litter discomfort warrants vet exam per AAFP guidelines

topic: Why cats hate water: ancestral desert adaptation explanation
key_points: Domestic cats descend from African wildcat (Felis lybica) with no water adaptation per 2017 Nature genetics
Coat is not waterproof and becomes heavy when wet, slowing the cat
Some breeds like Turkish Van and Bengal actually enjoy water
Sudden bathing creates stress, gradual exposure recommended per Cornell behaviorists

topic: Cat headbutting (bunting) science: marking you as safe property
key_points: Cats have scent glands on cheeks, forehead, and chin per Cornell Vet anatomy
Bunting deposits pheromones marking objects and people as familiar
Behavior is sign of trust and social bonding per ASPCA
Mutual bunting between cats reinforces colony group scent

topic: Cat in box phenomenon: stress reduction proven by Dutch 2014 study
key_points: Utrecht University 2014 study by Vinke et al gave shelter cats hiding boxes
Cats with boxes adapted faster to new environment with lower stress scores
Published in Applied Animal Behaviour Science journal
Boxes provide ambush vantage and security per feline ethology research

topic: Cat pheromones explained: Feliway synthetic facial pheromone uses
key_points: Feliway Classic mimics F3 facial pheromone per Ceva Animal Health research
Reduces stress markers like spraying, scratching, and hiding
Cornell behavior team confirmed efficacy in multi-cat households
Feliway Friends mimics F4 maternal pheromone, promotes bonding

topic: Why cats stare unblinking: predator focus and social communication
key_points: Direct unblinking stare in cats often signals threat or attention per ASPCA
Soft gaze with slow blinks indicates relaxation and affection per Sussex 2020
Pupil dilation during stare can mean fear, excitement, or low light
Avoid direct prolonged eye contact with unfamiliar cats per Cornell behavior

topic: Black cat folklore: bad luck in West, good luck in UK and Japan
key_points: Medieval Europe associated black cats with witchcraft after Pope Gregory IX 1233
UK considers crossing path of black cat lucky per cultural folklore studies
Japan views black cats as bringing love and prosperity
Black cats wait 30 percent longer in shelters per ASPCA adoption data

topic: Cat indoor enrichment: WSAVA five pillars of healthy environment
key_points: WSAVA five pillars: safe space, key resources, play, social interaction, scent respect
Vertical territory like cat trees reduces stress per ISFE feline environment guidelines
One litter box per cat plus one extra prevents inappropriate elimination
Daily play sessions of 10 to 15 minutes maintain physical and mental health

topic: Cat litter types compared: clay, silica, pine, wheat, paper
key_points: Clumping clay litter most preferred by cats in 2010 Cornell preference study
Silica gel absorbs more moisture but some cats reject the texture
Pine and wheat litters are biodegradable but less odor control
Avoid scented litters which can deter cats per AAFP environmental guidelines

topic: Cat wet vs dry food debate: hydration benefits of canned diet
key_points: Wet food contains 70 to 80 percent moisture vs 10 percent in dry per AAFCO
Higher moisture intake supports kidney and urinary tract health per Cornell Feline Health Center
Cats evolved to get most water from prey, not bowls
Mixed feeding wet plus dry common per Royal Canin nutrition guidelines

topic: Cat obesity epidemic: 60% overweight per APOP 2022 survey
key_points: Association for Pet Obesity Prevention 2022 survey: 61 percent of US cats overweight
Obesity raises risk of diabetes, joint disease, and shorter lifespan per Royal Canin research
Body Condition Score 5 of 9 is ideal per WSAVA chart
Restricted feeding plus measured portions reduce obesity per AVMA weight guidelines

topic: Cat diabetes type 2: Burmese breed predisposition and remission
key_points: Diabetes mellitus affects 0.5 to 1 percent of cats per Cornell Feline Health Center
Burmese breed has 3 to 4 times higher risk per Royal Canin breed predisposition data
Up to 80 percent achieve remission with insulin and low-carb diet per AAFP diabetes guidelines
Early treatment within 6 months gives best remission chance

topic: Cats bury poop: dominance hierarchy and predator avoidance theory
key_points: Burying waste hides scent from predators per Cornell behavior research
Subordinate cats bury feces, dominant feral cats may leave it exposed as territorial marker
Behavior adopted from African wildcat ancestor (Felis lybica)
Refusing to bury can signal litter aversion or medical issue per AAFP guidelines

topic: Cats pant rarely: open mouth breathing is veterinary emergency
key_points: Cats pant only briefly after extreme heat or exertion per Cornell Vet College
Persistent open-mouth breathing signals respiratory distress, heart failure, or asthma
Always emergency unlike normal canine panting
Feline asthma affects 1 to 5 percent of cats per AAFP respiratory guidelines

topic: Cat hairballs: how often is normal vs vet emergency
key_points: Occasional hairball every 1 to 2 weeks normal per Cornell Feline Health Center
More frequent vomiting may signal IBD, gastrointestinal issue, or excessive grooming
Long-haired breeds need brushing 3 to 5 times weekly to reduce hairballs
Hairball gels contain mineral oil to ease passage per AVMA recommendations

topic: Why cats hate citrus: D-limonene receptor sensitivity explained
key_points: Cats find citrus oils strongly aversive per Cornell behavior research
D-limonene in citrus is toxic to cats in concentrated form per ASPCA poison control
Some commercial cat repellents use citrus scent for outdoor barriers
Lemon and orange essential oils should never be applied to cats

topic: Cat paw preference: 2017 Queens University Belfast study
hook: Cats are right or left handed
key_points: Queens University Belfast 2017 study found cats show paw preference
Males tend to be left-pawed, females right-pawed per Wells and Millsopp research
Published in Animal Behaviour journal
Tested by reaching into food jar or stepping over barrier

topic: Why cats have 9 lives myth: ancient Egypt to British folklore
key_points: Number 9 considered mystical in ancient Egypt where cats were sacred to Bastet
British proverb dates from at least 1546 in John Heywood's collection
Some cultures cite 7 or 6 lives instead, like Spain and Turkey
Origin reflects cats' agility and survival skills, not actual lifespans

topic: Cat righting reflex: how cats land on feet from any angle
key_points: Righting reflex develops by 3 to 4 weeks of age per Cornell Vet anatomy
Vestibular apparatus in inner ear detects orientation
Flexible spine and lack of clavicle allow body rotation mid-fall
Paradoxically, falls under 2 stories often more dangerous than 7 stories per 1987 Annals of Veterinary research

topic: Bombay cat: a black panther in miniature, bred in Kentucky 1958
key_points: Bombay breed created by Nikki Horner of Kentucky starting 1958
Crossed Sable Burmese with black American Shorthair
All Bombay cats have all-black coats and copper-gold eyes per CFA standard
CFA accepted Bombay for championship in 1976

topic: Devon Rex: pixie face cat with hair like sheep wool from 1959
key_points: Devon Rex originated in Devonshire, England in 1959 from a curly-coated kitten named Kirlee
Coat caused by recessive gene, different from Cornish Rex gene
Large ears and impish face are signature features per CFA standard
Often tolerated better by allergy sufferers due to less guard hair shedding

topic: Cornish Rex: marcel-wave coat from a 1950 farm cat in Cornwall
key_points: Cornish Rex appeared in 1950 on a farm in Bodmin Moor, Cornwall
Has only soft down hair, lacking guard hairs of typical cats
Foundation kitten Kallibunker had recessive curly coat gene
Body is athletic and slim, sometimes called whippet-like per CFA

topic: Egyptian Mau: only naturally spotted breed dating to ancient Egypt
key_points: Egyptian Mau is the only natural domestic breed with spotted coat per CFA
Depicted in Egyptian art dating 1400 BCE
Modern breed re-established by Princess Natalie Troubetskoy in 1953
Fastest domestic breed, can run up to 48 km/h per FIFe data

topic: Abyssinian: oldest known cat breed appearing in 19th-century records
key_points: Abyssinian first shown at Crystal Palace cat show in 1871
Genetic studies suggest Indian Ocean coast origin, not Ethiopia per UC Davis
Ticked tabby coat with each hair banded with multiple colors
Gold or green almond-shaped eyes per CFA breed standard

topic: Russian Blue: silvery shimmer breed once a Tsar's gift to royalty
key_points: Russian Blue dates to Archangel Isles in northern Russia, 1860s
Coat has silver-tipped guard hairs creating shimmering effect
Vivid green eyes are signature feature per CFA breed standard
Said to be gifts from Tsars to European royalty

topic: Cat introduces self with butt presentation: scent gland identification
key_points: Anal scent glands carry individual identifying pheromones per Cornell Vet
Butt presentation is friendly greeting and trust signal
Sniffing rear is normal cat-to-cat social behavior
Behavior also functions as cat ID card in colony hierarchy

topic: Cats ignore commands not because dumb but because choice: 2009 study
key_points: University of Tokyo 2009 study confirmed cats recognize owner voice but choose response
Research published in Animal Cognition journal by Saito and Shinozuka
Cats lack pack hierarchy that drives canine obedience
Reward-based training works with cats per ASPCA training guides

topic: Cats produce 100+ vocalizations: dogs only 10
key_points: Cats can produce over 100 distinct vocalizations per Cornell Vet College
Dogs produce around 10 vocalizations for comparison
Adult cats meow primarily to communicate with humans, rarely to other cats
Wider vocal range developed during 9000 years of human cohabitation

topic: Why cats chatter at birds: predatory frustration or kill mimicry
key_points: Chattering occurs when cats see unreachable prey per Cornell behavior research
Theory by ethologist Brad Schwarz suggests it mimics killing bite movement
Adrenaline surge during prey sighting drives behavior
Behavior more common in indoor cats viewing outdoor birds

topic: Cat sleep stages: 70% light sleep ready to react in 1 second
key_points: Cats spend 12 to 16 hours sleeping daily per Cornell Vet College
About 70 percent is light sleep, allowing rapid wake response
Only 30 percent is deep REM sleep with twitching
Light sleep evolved as predator survival mechanism

topic: Cats more affectionate than thought: 2019 Oregon State attachment study
key_points: Oregon State University 2019 study found 65 percent of cats show secure attachment to humans
Comparable rates to dogs and human infants per Vitale and Udell research
Published in Current Biology journal
Used Strange Situation Test originally designed for human infants

topic: Cats see fewer colors: dichromatic vision similar to red-green colorblind
key_points: Cats are dichromatic with two cone types vs three in humans per UC Davis vision research
See blues and yellows but red-greens appear muted
Have 6 to 8 times more rods than humans, better night vision
Visual acuity peaks at 6 meters vs 30 meters in humans

topic: Cats need fountains: running water preference and dehydration risk
key_points: Cats prefer moving water over still per Cornell Feline Health Center
Stems from instinct that running water is fresher than stagnant
Adequate hydration prevents urinary disease and kidney issues
Cat fountains can increase water intake by 30 percent per Royal Canin studies

topic: Cat scratching post science: vertical vs horizontal preferences
key_points: Cats prefer scratching post 80 cm tall or taller per Cornell behavior research
Sisal rope is preferred surface in 2017 Wilbourn study from UC Davis
Scratching marks territory and sheds nail husks
Place posts near rest areas for stretching after waking

topic: Why declawing is illegal: amputation truth banned in 42 countries
key_points: Declawing (onychectomy) removes the last bone of each toe, equivalent to human fingertip amputation
Banned in 42 countries plus New York and Maryland per AVMA tracking
AVMA discourages declawing in favor of nail trimming and scratching posts
May lead to chronic pain, behavior issues, and litter box aversion per AAFP

topic: Toxic foods for cats: chocolate, onions, lilies, grapes lethal list
key_points: Chocolate contains theobromine, toxic to cats per ASPCA Animal Poison Control
Onion and garlic cause Heinz body anemia in red blood cells
All true lily species cause acute kidney failure even from pollen exposure
Grapes and raisins cause kidney failure with unknown toxic compound

topic: Cat parasites checklist: fleas, ticks, worms, ear mites identification
key_points: Fleas can lay 50 eggs per day, infesting home environment per Companion Animal Parasite Council
Roundworms (Toxocara cati) common in kittens, can transmit to humans
Ear mites (Otodectes cynotis) cause dark coffee-ground discharge
Year-round preventatives recommended per AAFP parasite guidelines

topic: Pancreatitis silent killer: 67% of cats have evidence at necropsy
key_points: Texas A&M studies found 67 percent of cats show pancreatitis evidence on necropsy
Symptoms vague: lethargy, decreased appetite, hiding
Spec fPL blood test diagnoses with 79 percent sensitivity per AAFP
Often co-occurs with IBD and cholangitis (triaditis)

topic: Cat heart disease: HCM affects 15% per Royal Veterinary College
key_points: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) most common feline heart disease per Royal Veterinary College
Affects 15 percent of cats and up to 30 percent of Maine Coons per UC Davis genetics
Echocardiogram is gold standard for diagnosis
MYBPC3 gene mutation causes HCM in Maine Coons and Ragdolls per Cornell genetics

topic: Cat pregnancy 63 days: gestation timeline week by week
key_points: Average feline gestation is 63 to 65 days per Cornell Feline Health Center
Pink nipples (pinking up) visible by week 3
Average litter size 4 kittens, range 1 to 9 per ASPCA reproduction guide
Ultrasound confirms pregnancy from day 21, x-ray from day 45

topic: How cats find their way home: 3-mile homing range studied 1922
key_points: 1922 Frances Herrick study tracked cat homing up to 3 miles
Cats use magnetic field detection per 2014 University of Kansas research
Visual landmarks and scent trails also contribute to navigation
Always microchip cats per AVMA recommendation, even indoor pets

topic: Spaying before first heat reduces cancer risk by 91%
key_points: Spaying before first heat reduces mammary tumor risk by 91 percent per AVMA data
First heat occurs around 5 to 9 months in domestic cats
AAFP recommends spay or neuter by 5 months of age
Eliminates risk of pyometra, a life-threatening uterine infection

topic: Cat breed personality myths: science-backed temperament differences
key_points: University of Helsinki 2019 study surveyed 5726 cats across 19 breeds
Found measurable behavioral differences between breeds, mostly genetic
Russian Blue scored most shy, Burmese most sociable
Published in Scientific Reports, confirms heritability of cat personality

topic: Cats can suffer depression: signs and treatment options
key_points: Cats experience grief and depression after loss of companion or owner per Cornell Vet
Signs include reduced appetite, lethargy, hiding, vocalization changes
Environmental enrichment and gradual routine support recovery per ASPCA
Severe cases benefit from fluoxetine prescription per AAFP behavior guidelines

topic: Hamilton the hipster cat: handlebar mustache cat with 700K followers
key_points: Hamilton the Hipster Cat had distinctive handlebar mustache marking
Reached over 700000 Instagram followers at peak
Owned by Mitch and Heather, lived in San Francisco
Featured in Vogue, BuzzFeed and earned merchandise revenue

topic: Cat colony social hierarchy: matriarchal feral cat structure
key_points: Feral colonies organize around related females per UC Davis ethology research
Mothers, daughters, and sisters share kitten care collectively
Males roam between colonies, not part of core group
Breaks myth that cats are solitary, social structure is flexible

topic: Cat tongue spines (papillae): 290 backward keratin barbs cleaning fur
key_points: Cat tongue has 290 sharp keratin papillae per 2018 Georgia Tech study
Hollow scoop shape transfers saliva to fur for grooming
Allows cats to strip meat from bones efficiently
Published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS)

topic: Cats can be trained: clicker training and tricks possible at any age
key_points: Cats learn through positive reinforcement using clicker training per ASPCA
Behaviors like sit, high-five, target touch achievable in 2-4 sessions
Short 3 to 5 minute sessions optimal for cat focus per AAFP
Treat-based reward more effective than verbal praise per Cornell behavior

topic: Cats live longer than ever: average lifespan jumped from 7 to 15 years
key_points: Average indoor cat lifespan now 13 to 17 years per Cornell Vet College
1980s data showed average 7 years due to outdoor risks and lack of veterinary care
Vaccines, parasite prevention, and dental care drive increase per AVMA
Oldest verified cat Creme Puff reached 38 years per Guinness records 2005
